commitResults.messageTitle: Implement batch command, organize Ui printing and Codebase
commitResults.messageBody:
The application currently lacks a way to add and track specific
batches of inventory items. Additionally, print statements are
scattered across different classes, and the centralized parsing
logic will become harder to manage as we add more features.

Introducing the batch feature requires a solid foundation for
parsing its specific parameters. At the same time, moving output
handling strictly to the Ui class ensures a clean separation of
concerns, making the code much easier to read and maintain.

Let's,
* implement BatchCommand to handle adding batches with expiry dates
* update InventoryItem to track batchQuantity and getQuantity
* migrate scattered print statements into dedicated Ui class methods
* extract prepareBatchCommand and prepareCreateCommand in Parser
* update the Command interface to accept Ui and Inventory parameters
* implement the exit command and resolve the early exit bug
* extract helper functions from the main loop in the MediStock class

Centralizing output in the Ui class keeps our logic decoupled from
user presentation. Structuring the batch implementation this way,
along with the updated Command interface, provides a highly modular
template for any future commands we add.
